data.title: Egress: verbatim-span-from-context echo detection in broker

data.body: Deferred from NAVI-6 (egress hardening). Spec 6.2 names 'long verbatim spans copied from the model's context' as an exfil class to deny, but _egress_check(value: str) only sees the outbound tool arg, not the run context. Implementing this requires threading the run's context text (user message and/or prior tool outputs) into broker() and _egress_check -- a SIGNATURE CHANGE across all callers (loop.py + eval/unit tests). Design questions to resolve in planning: what counts as a 'span' (n-gram length, normalization), whether to include prior tool outputs, and the false-positive surface (a model legitimately quoting a KB snippet title into a follow-up web_search is normal research behavior). See the # TODO(scope) left in src/navi/broker.py _egress_check docstring. Well-understood approach, non-trivial -> backlog.

REVIEW (agent:claude-fable-5-reviewer, cold, commit b115066 on dev).

REVIEWED: plan (6 decisions + 7 acceptance criteria), full diff (broker.py / contracts.py / loop.py / test_broker.py / test_eval_egress.py), final _verbatim_span + _egress_check, run_loop corpus assembly. Traced the contiguous-extension loop and the corpus-aliasing path empirically.

CHECKS: ruff -> 'All checks passed!'; mypy -> 'Success: no issues found in 36 source files'; scoped pytest (tests/test_broker.py tests/test_eval_egress.py) -> 47 passed, <1s. Full suite NOT re-run (already passed 136/1-skip/0-fail; DB down per NAVI-21 makes it ~6.5min, environmental/unrelated).

PER-CRITERION:
1. broker() signature UNCHANGED, corpus rides on RunContext.egress_context (default empty tuple) — PASS. No broker() caller changed shape (loop.py call at line 141 unchanged; only ctx.egress_context assigned before it).
2. _egress_check/_verbatim_span PURE/deterministic, no I/O; verbatim branch runs LAST under 'if corpus:', no-ops on empty corpus — PASS. Pre-existing egress tests UNMODIFIED in diff (only removed line in test_broker.py is the import; zero removed lines in test_eval_egress.py) — CONFIRMED.
3. Threshold >=16 tokens AND >=80 chars — PASS. Verified: exact-16 trips (returns 16), 15 returns None, 16 tiny tokens (47 chars) returns None (char gate holds), short title quote stays Allowed, unrelated query Allowed.
4. Corpus = user_text + prior knowledge_base_search outputs ONLY; corpus.extend gated strictly under 'if tu.name == knowledge_base_search' — PASS. web_search outputs excluded.
5. Deny traced by TOKEN COUNT only; reason = 'verbatim span copied from context (N tokens)'; deny test asserts raw span absent from repr(records[-1]) — PASS.
6. ruff + mypy clean, thresholds typed module-level constants, scoped pytest green — PASS.
7. (=accept #6/#7) TODO(scope) rewritten: verbatim DONE; fuzzy/semantic/edit-distance OUT, prior web_search outputs excluded, verbatim trace column deferred — PASS.

ADVERSARIAL:
- Off-by-one: extension loop ('for j in range(i+n, len(q)+1) ... else: j=len(q)+1; end=j-1; run=q[i:end]') is CORRECT. No false miss at 16, no trip at 15 (verified empirically). The for/else handles the run-to-end-of-query case cleanly.
- BYPASS (accepted): inserting one word mid-span -> None (drops contiguous run below 16); changing one token only breaks the prefix, a 17-token tail still trips. Exact-n-gram evasion is real but EXPLICITLY documented as deferred in the TODO(scope) (fuzzy/semantic OUT) — not silently claimed airtight. SEVERITY: low/accepted-by-design.
- FALSE POSITIVE: a legit web_search reusing >=16 contiguous verbatim words from the user's own long question IS denied (returns 23 on the test span). JUDGMENT: acceptable. The deny is surfaced to the model (payload 'DENIED by tool broker: ...'), so the model reformulates rather than failing silently; 16 contiguous in-order tokens / 80+ chars is not idiomatic for a freshly composed query. Not over-aggressive for a read-only security boundary that fails toward denial.
- CORPUS ALIASING: 'ctx.egress_context = tuple(corpus)' snapshots an immutable tuple before each broker call; 'corpus' list grows only via extend() inside the KB branch — no aliasing, web_search genuinely excluded. CONFIRMED clean. Minor note (non-blocking): a KB hit and a web_search in the SAME resp.tool_uses batch — the web_search sees corpus only up to the prior turn (snapshot taken before the KB extend in iteration order). Conservative/correct; the realistic exfil window (user text + prior-turn KB) is covered.

VERDICT: PASS
